project.title: Proteomic Characterization of Naiavirus
project.projectDescription: Giant viruses infecting amoebae are notable for their substantial particle sizes and expansive genomes. In this study, we collected water samples from 44 distinct locations along a river within Brazil's Pantanal biome. These samples were processed and subsequently inoculated into Vermamoeba vermiformis cultures. Observation of cytopathic effects led to the isolation of a novel virus, designated Naiavirus, distinguished by its unprecedented virion morphology and genomic organization. To elucidate the protein composition of Naiavirus particles, large-scale infections were conducted using 20 T175 flasks, each containing 10 million amoebae. Post-infection, viral particles were purified through two successive sucrose gradient centrifugations to achieve high purity. Proteomic analysis of these purified particles identified 254 associated viral proteins, a count exceeding those reported for all known giant viruses except for pandoraviruses. This dataset encompasses the raw mass spectrometry data, identification files, and associated metadata, offering valuable insights into the structural proteome of this unique giant virus.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: 100 million purified Naiavirus particles were resuspended in Laemmli buffer (90 mM DTT, 2% SDS, 80 mM Tris-HCl pH 6.8, 10% glycerol, 0.1% bromophenol blue) to extract and solubilize proteins. The protein mixture was briefly separated by SDS-PAGE, halted upon entry into the resolving gel. The gel was stained with Coomassie Brilliant Blue, and the single band containing all proteins was excised. In-gel digestion involved destaining with 50% methanol and 2.5% acetic acid, reduction with 10 mM DTT, alkylation with 50 mM iodoacetamide, and digestion with 1 µg of trypsin at 37°C for 16 hours. Peptides were extracted using 5% formic acid in 50% acetonitrile, desalted with StageTips containing C18 Empore disks, dried, and reconstituted in 10 µL of 0.1% formic acid for LC-MS/MS analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Mass spectrometry data were acquired using an Orbitrap Exploris 240 coupled to an EASY-nLC 1200 system. Peptides were separated over an 80-minute gradient (2–40% acetonitrile) at 250 nL/min using a trap Acclaim PepMap 100 nanoViper C18 column (2 cm × 75 μm, 3 μm) and an analytical PepMap RSLC C18 column (50 cm × 75 μm, 2 μm). MS1 spectra (m/z 375–1500) were acquired at 60,000 resolution, followed by data-dependent MS/MS of the top 20 ions using HCD at 27% normalized collision energy. Dynamic exclusion was set to 20 seconds. Data analysis was performed with MaxQuant v2.4.7.0 using the Andromeda search engine against a concatenated database of Acanthamoeba castellanii and Naiavirus six-frame translations (37,160 sequences). Fixed modification: carbamidomethylation (C); variable modifications: methionine oxidation and N-terminal acetylation. Trypsin/P specificity allowed up to two missed cleavages. Mass tolerances were set to 10 ppm for precursors and 0.02 Da for fragments. False discovery rate (FDR) was controlled at 1% for both peptides and proteins. Label-free quantification (LFQ) was enabled with a minimum ratio count of one. Protein groups identified as contaminants, reverse hits, or only by site were excluded. LFQ intensities were log2-transformed using Perseus v2.0.11 for downstream analysis.

references.referenceLine: Rodrigues M, Queiroz V, Arantes T, Limborço H, Neiva B, Arias N, Machado T, Barcelos M, Cortines JR, Thiemann OH, Marques RE, Melo-Hanchuk TD, Hurtado Celis EL, Araujo JP, Reis E, Junior Alcantara LC, Santos CBC, Jivaji A, Rodrigues RAL, Aylward FO, Abrahão JS. Naiavirus: an enveloped giant virus with a pleomorphic, flexible tail. Nat Commun. 2025 16(1):8306

Characteristics[Specimen]: 108 viral particles were resuspended in Laemmli buffer (90 mM DTT, 2% SDS, 80 mM Tris-HCl pH 6.8, 10% glycerol, 0.1% bromophenol blue) for protein extraction and solubilization.
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Exploris 240 mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA)
Comment[Separation]: Acclaim PepMap 100 nanoViper 2PK C18 (2 cm × ID75 μm, 3 μm particle size, Thermo Scientific) in line with an analytical PepMap RSLC C18 ES 902 column (50 cm × id 75 μm, 2 μm particle size)
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: a maximum of two missed cleavages for trypsin/P
Comment[FractionationMethod]: purified twice, sequentially in a sucrose gradient
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: The 20 most intense peptide ions, with charge states of 2 or higher, were sequentially isolated to a target value of 3e5 and fragmented
